_comment: Per-MCU hardware facts. Shared by docs/index.html (fetch) and .github/scripts/gen_overlay.py (file read) so the two can never drift. A module in modules.json only names its 'mcu'; everything below is derived from the silicon, not the board. Adding a board that uses an existing MCU needs no change here.

nrf52840.label: nRF52840
nrf52840._ain: SAADC analog input mapping. Product Spec v1.8 table 'Analog inputs'. modules.json's analog_pins is computed as (module pins) INTERSECT (these keys) -- never hand-maintained.
nrf52840._clock_pins: null = the SPIM/TWIM clock output may sit on any GPIO.
nrf52840._port_constraints: {} = every signal may sit on any port.
nrf52840._rx_follows_tx_port: false = UART RX and TX may live on different ports.

nrf54l15.label: nRF54L15
nrf54l15._ain: SAADC analog input mapping. Note this is NOT the same as the nRF52's -- the nRF54L's AIN0..7 are on P1.04-P1.07 and P1.11-P1.14.
nrf54l15._clock_pins: The nRF54L's SPIM/TWIM clock output can only be driven from these pads.
nrf54l15._port_constraints: Which GPIO ports each signal may use. The high-speed serial instances (spi20/i2c21/spi22) are wired to port 1 only; slower signals and the port-0 UART (uart30) may also use port 0.
nrf54l15._rx_follows_tx_port: true = TX and RX must share a port, because the port decides the UART instance (port 0 -> uart30, port 1 -> uart22).
